meaning: n.颤声,抖音,啭声vt.用抖动的声音说,用卷舌发音vi.发出抖动的声音
examples: It was a real trill to meet her.
Just trill back, "Oh, you're a keen gardener!"
Their song consists mainly of a rapidly repeated note, like a trill.
The timbre, the trill, the resonant frequency of the Cayman is now much closer to the premium-feeling 911.
Lead singer Lizzie Bougatsos was a vision in red, beating a drum and charming like a siren in her high-pitched trill.
